CONTENT.Club career: Following two loans to HNK Vukovar '91 in Croatia's 2. HNL and FC Honka in Finland's Veikkausliiga, Vargić made his debut with NK Osijek in 1. HNL in April 2009. He earned 36 league caps before he left the club in 2013.On 5 February 2013, HNK Rijeka announced that they have signed Vargić on a 4+1⁄2-year contract. During the 2015–16 1. HNL season, Vargić broke two Rijeka records: the longest streak without conceding a goal and goalkeeper who kept most clean sheets in one season. From 2 August 2015 to 4 October 2015, he did not concede a goal for 783 consecutive minutes, which is the second longest streak in the history of 1. HNL. During the 2015–16 1. HNL season, he conceded only 13 goals in 32 appearances, keeping 21 clean sheets. With 23 appearances, Vargić is also the club's most capped goalkeeper in Europe.On 1 February 2016, both Croatian and Italian press reported that Lazio have signed Vargić for a fee of €2.7 million. Lazio immediately sent Vargić on loan to Rijeka until 30 June 2016.On 3 September 2018, Vargić joined Cypriot club Anorthosis Famagusta on a season-long loan.On 6 February 2020, he joined Koper in Slovenia on loan until the end of the 2019–20 season.
CONTENT.External links: Ivan Vargić at Soccerway.comIvan Vargić at National-Football-Teams.comIvan Vargić at FBref.comIvan Vargić at the Croatian Football FederationIvan Vargić at EU-Football.infoIvan Vargić at Croatian Football Statistics  (archived) (in Croatian)
CONTENT.International career: Vargić was a member of Croatia under-21 national team. On 12 November 2014, he made his senior national team debut in a friendly game against Argentina, entering as a substitute for Lovre Kalinić. Vargić went on to cap his national side for two more times, and the last match of his national career being against Northern Ireland in 2016.

SUMMARY: Ivan Vargić (Croatian pronunciation: [ǐʋan ʋâːrɡitɕ]; born 15 March 1987) is a Croatian footballer who plays as a goalkeeper for Krk. He had played for Slovenian club FC Koper in the years before returning to Croatia.
